feed.title: Civic Autonomy
feed.subtitle: Decentralised architecture for autonomous, empathetic communities.

entry.title: The Welfare Trap
entry.content: The myth of the “benefit scrounger” masks a structural failure of disenfranchisement. Centralized welfare systems are engineered to manage regional neglect through dependency loops, rather than using decentralized frameworks to foster local economic autonomy and useful civic participation.
entry.summary: Moving from punitive centralized control to localized civic productivity.

entry.title: Hospital Waiting Times
entry.content: We treat healthcare as a massive, top-down bureaucratic monolith. The system rewards crisis management at massive centralized hubs (hospitals) instead of investing in the data-driven, localized, preventative civic health infrastructure needed to prevent the bottleneck in the first place.
entry.summary: Why the NHS crisis is a classic scale failure and centralised bottleneck.

entry.title: The Immigration Myth
entry.content: The hyper-fixation on small boat crossings is the ultimate political distraction machine. It focuses entirely on the final point of physical impact while ignoring the root cause: a total lack of localized economic stability and decentralized global resilience that makes communities vulnerable to external shocks.
entry.summary: Looking past the small boats to diagnose the systemic collapse of localized economic resilience.
